intro: Schizanthus gracilis, commonly known as the wild angelica or angelica of the Andes, is a flowering plant native to South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in relieving symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Schizanthus gracilis include volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat coughs, sore throats, and other upper respiratory tract infections.
